title: alpinia novae-hiberniae
intro: Alpinia novae-hiberniae, also known as the New Hebrides ginger, is a species of flowering plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) native to the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Alpinia novae-hiberniae include relief from nausea, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Alpinia novae-hiberniae is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional Asian medicine to treat a range of ailments.
